name: Omelet Mediterraneo
recipeIngredient: 1 small red or green bell pepper, seeded and chopped
1 cup egg substitutes or 4 large eggs, beaten
1 tablespoon mixture of dried oregano and basil
2 ounces crumbled feta cheese, up to 3
recipeInstructions:
Makes 2 servings
1. Combine the pepper, tomato, garlic, olives, and scallions in a small mixing bowl. Set aside.
2. In another small bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, and herbs until well combined.
3. Heat a lightly sprayed nonstick skillet or omelet pan over medium heat.
4. Pour about one-half of the egg mixture into the skillet and swirl with a spatula to spread evenly across the pan's surface.
5. Reduce the heat to low and cook for 1 to 2 minutes, until the edges are set but the surface is still moist.
6. Slide a spatula under the omelet and gently ease it onto a plate.
7. Add one-half of the vegetable mixture (bell pepper, tomato, garlic, olives, scallions) to half of the cooked omelet, forming a half moon shape.
8. Sprinkle about 1 tablespoon of crumbled feta cheese over the vegetable filling and gently fold the other half of the omelet over.
9. Cover the pan with a lid and cook for an additional 1 minute until the cheese is melted and lightly browned.
10. Turn off the heat, let the omelet sit in the pan for another minute, then slide it onto a warm plate using a spatula.
11. Re-season the pan with vegetable spray and repeat the process with the remaining ingredients.
12. Serve with Rosemary Roasted Sweet Potatoes or Spinach Spuds.
